title: Are default-policy.json & default.yaml in wrong place? Shouldn't they be in containers/image?
body:
According to Fedora's source [spec](https://src.fedoraproject.org/rpms/containers-common/blob/rawhide/f/containers-common.spec), various "common" files are pulled from containers/{common,image,storage,shortnames} which makes sense as they're all modules. But these 2 common files are pulled from skopeo which already depends on containers/common, so it doesn't make sense to have both in skopeo.

All major distros (Fedora, debian, archlinux, gentoo, alpine, opensuse) are placing these 2 files in containers-common package.
Thoughts on moving them to containers/image?

comments.body:
I have no opinion on the location of these files, I’ll fully defer to our packaging experts (and this _must_ be coordinated with them).

@lsm5 @jnovy PTAL.
The 2 files in question are now in c/image.
@lsm5 the Skopeo repo still contains copies. Can we remove them?

> @lsm5 the Skopeo repo still contains copies. Can we remove them?

The containers-common rpm [fetches them from c/image](https://github.com/containers/common/blob/main/rpm/containers-common.spec#L66-L67) . So it should be safe to remove unless other tests need it.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
